name: Stop the Beach Renourishment Inc. v. Florida Department of Environmental Protection
question: By reversing longstanding holdings that littoral (i.e., on or near the shore) rights are constitutionally protected, did the Florida Supreme Court cause a "judicial taking" proscribed by the Fifth and Fourteenth Amendments?
description: A case in which the Court held that a ruling on littoral ("on or near the shore") takings by the Florida Supreme Court did not cause a judicial takings violation of the Fifth or Fourteenth Amendments.
